title: Sexual Assault/Incest Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Sexual Assault/Incest Support Groups 27 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community CALL TO SAFETY SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emotional support, safety planning, crisis intervention, information and referrals, and education through the support line. In-person hospital response for sexual assault is also available through the Unified Response Group. PO Box 42610 Portland, OR 97242 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or violence Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 235-5333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sexual assault and domestic violence support groups. 2208 SW 3rd Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Meeting times vary Phone hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 754-0110 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of domestic violence, sexual assault, and human trafficking survivor support services such as support groups, 911 cell phones, court accompaniment, community education, the Address Confidentiality Program, and information and referral to other social services. 605 Center Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual abuse, human trafficking, and/or stalking, regardless of gender identity or sexual orientation Hours: Walk in: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 399-7722 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WORKS SEXUAL ASSAULT SURVIVOR ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advocacy for survivors of sexual assault, including accompaniment to SANE exams, safety planning, resource navigation, emotional support, and systems advocacy, including support groups. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Any individual who has experienced sexual assault, including stalking Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 276-3322 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, protective order assistance, emergency shelter, safety planning, court and medical accompaniment, referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 726 N 1st Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, dating violence, human trafficking, and elder abuse. Also serves individulas with disabilities who are victims of abuse. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 947-2449 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, safety planning, short-term shelter, counseling, court advocacy, support groups, and referral to and advocacy with local agencies and other programs that offer long-term shelter. For survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault, provides assistance with temporary restraining and stalking orders. Does not provide intervention services for perpetrators of violence. 420 E 3rd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -Hours vary for other services Email (541) 296-1662 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE HOTLINE AND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline and a 30-day emergency shelter with six rooms for survivors of sexual assault, domestic violence, or stalking. Offers medical and legal advocacy, including accompaniment to appointments and assistance with paperwork. Food, clothing, and peer counseling are available as well. PO Box 441 Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Anyone fleeing sexual assault, domestic violence, or stalking Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-6603 247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HENDERSON HOUSE HENDERSON HOUSE ADVOCACY CENTER CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic violence and sexual assault client advocacy services. Services include a crisis hotline, coordinated wraparound service, referrals, assistance with filling out protective orders, youth outreach support groups, weekly support groups for domestic violence and sexual violence survivors (including adult survivors of child sexual abuse), and an art therapy group. 610 SE 1st Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Survivors of interpersonal and/or sexual violence (including stalking), human trafficking, and their children who have experienced or witnessed abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Support groups: Tuesday/ Thursday 6pm-7:30pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 472-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a range of advocacy and support to survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault. Services include crisis intervention, court and medical accompaniment, peer advocacy, support groups, assistance with locating financial help, assistance with restraining and stalking orders, community education, referrals to meet emergency needs, and emergency shelter. 103 S Kerby Avenue Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, closed federal holidays Email (541) 592-2515 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a helpline, support groups, and a variety of supportive services for survivors of intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, and trafficking. Provides emergency shelter services, based on availability, for survivors. Also provides in-person response to the emergency room for sexual assault survivors and assistance with protective and stalking orders. Every person who contacts Klamath Advocacy Center gets five essential services for safety and independence: safety plan, risk assessment, needs assessment, resource information, and next steps. PO Box 1358 Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Shelter: Women and women with minor children who are survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence or sexual assault All other services: Survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence, sexual assault, dating violence, and/or stalking Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 884-0390 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic and sexual violence services, including safety planning, crisis intervention, advocacy, and educational services. Works with local programs to facilitate safety planning, provide transportation as necessary, and ensure the delivery of culturally relevant services in local shelters. Weekly domestic violence support groups that are closed groups held off-site for confidentiality reasons. Free child care is available. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence, primarily serves those who self-identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm. Support group times vary. Email (503) 288-8177 339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEACE AT HOME ADVOCACY CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SHELTER AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, emergency shelter, support groups, and legal advocacy (assistance with protective and stalking orders). 1202 SE Douglas Avenue Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing domestic abuse, sexual assault, human trafficking, or stalking. Shelter allows pets. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 673-7867 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support groups and community education on sexual harassment, sexual assault, and domestic violence and adult domestic violence prevention workshops. Provides medical, legal, and financial advocacy for survivors such as navigating the Department of Human Services Child Welfare and the Self Sufficiency office. Assists with applying for Temporary Assistance for Domestic Violence Survivors (TA-DVS). Rural outreach: Supports survivors with safety planning, support groups, and meets for weekly consultation. 2194 Columbia Boulevard Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and/or sexual violence, stalking, and sex trafficking Hours: Varies Email (503) 397-6161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE PROJECT THE SAFE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter for domestic violence and sexual assault survivors. Also provides a crisis line, outreach services, support groups, and community education. 1681 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Coos Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Curry Outreach Office Tuesday - Friday 8am to 4pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 756-7000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. 990 NW Brooks Street Suite 1 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 389-7021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 475-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE PRINEVILLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 416-2114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE REDMOND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 504-2550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SELF ENHANCEMENT INC SELF ENHANCEMENT INC COMMUNITY & FAMILY PROGRAMS ANTI DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers safe and confidential services to survivors of domestic and sexual violence including advocacy, case management, support groups, safety planning, relocation assistance, emergency, medical and judicial accompaniments, and referrals to other resources. 310 NE Failing Street Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week. Calls placed outside of Monday-Friday 9am-5pm and holidays are forwarded to Call to Safety. Email (503) 972-3698 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEXUAL ASSAULT RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-person and telephone accompaniment to hospitals in Washington County, law enforcement agencies, schools, clinics, shelters, and other secure locations, supporting individuals during medical exams, law enforcement interviews, and other related situations. Limited case management services including ongoing emotional support, safety planning, assistance through criminal and civil justice processes, address confidentiality, crime victim compensation, housing advocacy, protection orders, and referrals to additional resources. Also offers limited peer-led support and educational groups, as well as prevention education and community presentations. 100 NE Campton Drive Hillsboro, OR 97006 Eligibility: Individuals who live, work, attend school, or had sexual assault incident occur in Washington County Hours: Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services: No standard hours Email (503) 640-5311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for survivors of past and present sexual assault, abuse, and harassment and for friends and family of survivors. Services include a crisis line, medical response and legal advocacy, support groups, peer support, community education and outreach for prevention, and information and referral. All services are confidential. 1902 Jefferson Street Suite 1 Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault, abuse, and harassment, and their friends and family members Hours: -Crisis line, advocacy, legal, and medical response: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -All other services: Hours vary (844) 404-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHELTER FROM THE STORM LA GRANDE - SHELTER FROM THE STORM DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a crisis line and emergency shelter. Advocacy includes information and referral, assistance with restraining orders, reporting options, counseling and support groups, and other supportive services. 10901 Island Avenue Island City, OR 97850 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence or sexual assault and their families and supporters Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 963-7226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CALL TO SAFETY SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT GROUPS CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY SERVICES COMMUNITY WORKS SEXUAL ASSAULT SURVIVOR ADVOCACY DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE HOTLINE AND EMERGENCY SERVICES HENDERSON HOUSE CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM PEACE AT HOME ADVOCACY CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SHELTER AND SUPPORT SERVICES SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SUPPORT GROUPS SAFE PROJECT THE SAFE PROJECT SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE SELF ENHANCEMENT INC ANTI DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE PROGRAM SEXUAL ASSAULT RESOURCE CENTER SEXUAL ASSAULT RESOURCE CENTER SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES SHELTER FROM THE STORM DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES
